purpose: Design, test, and lint modular skills for OpenClaw-style agents.
metadata: Frontmatter with name and description; repository includes README.md, SKILL.md, references/, and scripts/skill_lint.py.
trigger_contract: Targets requests to design, review, or lint a skill; explicitly excludes marketplace management, dedicated security scanning, and normal skill use.
instructions: Stepwise workflow for trigger design, repo layout, SKILL drafting, linting, trigger tests, end-to-end testing, and QA review.
context_boundary: Treats SKILL.md as runtime context and moves detailed material into references/ to control context size.
execution_constraints: Requires Gotchas and Hard Stop sections; keeps SKILL.md under 500 lines; uses scripts for deterministic checks.
memory_interface: No persistent store; discusses fold-and-forget behavior and when hard constraints should move to system prompt.
tests: Static lint plus positive and negative trigger tests and an end-to-end skill run are described.
security_checks: Linter scans for sensitive strings and structural issues; repo defers dedicated security auditing to skill-security-guard.
failure_modes: Over-broad descriptions; version mismatch; missing Gotchas; missing Hard Stop; syntax errors; extra documentation files.
notes: Manually profiled from inspected public repo files on 2026-04-28; not executed in this benchmark repo.

purpose: Statically review skills for security risks before installation or use.
metadata: Frontmatter with name, version, description, and tags; repository includes detection rules and a scan entry script.
trigger_contract: Targets requests to scan skill code, SKILL.md files, or zip packages for security risks; excludes runtime monitoring.
instructions: Structured scan workflow covering input parsing, seven risk dimensions, scoring, reporting, whitelisting, and limited auto-fix.
context_boundary: Operates on uploaded skill text or archives rather than live runtime behavior.
execution_constraints: Hard Stop after repeated tool failures; scan scope is static and pre-deployment; session-scoped whitelist only.
memory_interface: No persistent memory interface; whitelist behavior is scoped to the current scan session.
tests: Repo documents single-file and batch scanning flows, but no executable regression corpus was inspected.
security_checks: Primary artifact for prompt injection, sensitive file access, malicious scripts, dependency risk, and trigger reasonability checks.
failure_modes: False positives on benign network use; missing rule files; archive extraction failures; broad descriptions misread as intent.
notes: Manually profiled from README.md, SKILL.md, references/detection-rules.md, and scripts/scan.sh.

purpose: Provide long-term agent memory through daily logs, distilled facts, and a compact session memory file.
metadata: Frontmatter with name, version, description, and tags; repository includes MEMORY and USER templates plus scripts/memory_manager.py.
trigger_contract: Targets explicit remember, recall, setup, and memory-health requests; excludes group chats, non-owner contexts, and one-off tasks.
instructions: Documents setup, daily digestion, weekly distillation, explicit remember flows, recall flows, and health checks.
context_boundary: Separates raw logs, structured facts, and compact session memory to limit active context size.
execution_constraints: Owner-only memory operations; no silent deletion; archive old logs; MEMORY.md constrained to 80 lines.
memory_interface: Three-layer interface: daily markdown logs, facts.yaml, and MEMORY.md, with archive and health support.
tests: Init and health commands create a natural smoke-test path; repo also describes trigger examples and failure handling.
security_checks: Security is mostly operational: owner-only use, no third-party forwarding, no silent destructive cleanup.
failure_modes: Stale memory; path mismatches; broken facts.yaml; overlong MEMORY.md; writing event data to the wrong layer.
notes: Manually profiled from README.md, SKILL.md, and scripts/memory_manager.py; no benchmark execution performed.

purpose: Run operational health checks on a deployed agent and its skill environment.
metadata: Frontmatter with name, version, description, and tags; repository includes audit criteria and a quick-check shell script.
trigger_contract: Targets explicit self-audit, system optimization, health-check, and memory-optimization requests; excludes skill code security scanning.
instructions: Eight ordered audit checks plus a lightweight heartbeat mode for quick periodic inspections.
context_boundary: Reads multiple operational files but is scoped to maintenance and diagnosis rather than task execution.
execution_constraints: Write operations require explicit user confirmation; quick-scan mode reports status without modifying state.
memory_interface: No standalone memory store; reads MEMORY.md and facts.yaml health as inputs to its audit.
tests: Quick-check script covers memory length, skill count, and facts.yaml presence; audit criteria provide thresholds.
security_checks: Security posture is procedural: confirm before writes, avoid deleting active capabilities blindly, check references before cleanup.
failure_modes: Miscounting skills; overlong MEMORY.md; missing cron prompts; leaking config detail during reports; stale facts data.
notes: Manually profiled from README.md, SKILL.md, references/audit-criteria.md, and scripts/health_check.sh.

purpose: Protect agent identity and privacy in multi-user or group-chat settings.
metadata: Frontmatter with name, version, description, and tags; repository centers on SKILL.md plus an identity-verification reference.
trigger_contract: Targets requests to configure identity protection, owner verification, privacy rules, or group-chat isolation; excludes whole-system security scans.
instructions: Provides a configuration snippet, three-tier identity logic, refusal policy, prompt-injection defenses, and setup guidance.
context_boundary: Pushes hard rules into SOUL.md or AGENTS.md so identity checks do not depend on ordinary skill triggering.
execution_constraints: Defaults to blocking sensitive operations for uncertain and non-owner cases; relies on trusted metadata.
memory_interface: No persistent store of its own; instead it constrains access to SOUL.md, USER.md, MEMORY.md, and related resources.
tests: No executable test harness was inspected; repo provides boundary cases and setup examples in documentation.
security_checks: Primary artifact for identity verification, capability non-disclosure, and privacy-preserving refusals.
failure_modes: Missing owner ID replacement; metadata ambiguity; false rejects in group chats; capability leakage if refusals are too explicit.
notes: Manually profiled from README.md, SKILL.md, and references/identity-verification.md; configuration-oriented evidence only.
